title: Fesenjān (Pomegranate Walnut Stew)
description: A rich, iconic Persian stew made with toasted ground walnuts, pomegranate molasses, and tender chicken, striking a perfect balance of sweet and sour.

ingredients.note: Boneless and skinless, or bone-in pieces
Optional, depending on sourness of molasses

instructions.text: Place the walnuts in a food processor and pulse until they are very finely ground, almost like a paste. Be careful not to turn it into walnut butter.
In a large pot, heat 1 tbsp of oil. Sauté the grated onion until soft and translucent. Add the ground walnuts and toast on low heat for 5-10 minutes, stirring constantly to prevent burning.

Add 3 cups of cold water to the walnuts. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to the lowest setting, cover, and simmer for 1 to 1.5 hours. To help the walnuts release their natural oils, drop an ice cube into the pot every 30 minutes.
While the walnut sauce is simmering, season the chicken pieces with salt, black pepper, and turmeric. In a skillet, heat 1 tbsp of oil and lightly brown the chicken on all sides. Set aside.
Stir the pomegranate molasses, sugar (if using), and cinnamon into the simmering walnut sauce. The sauce will darken.

Add the browned chicken to the walnut-pomegranate sauce. Cover and simmer gently for another 45-60 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and tender, and the sauce is thick and oil has pooled on top.
Taste the stew and adjust the sweetness/sourness with more sugar or pomegranate molasses as desired. Serve hot.

tips: The secret to a great Fesenjan is patience. Slow, low cooking is required to extract the oils from the walnuts. Adding ice water shocks the walnuts and helps them release oil.
storage_notes: Fesenjan tastes even better the next day. Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 5 days. It also freezes exceptionally well for up to 3 months.
serve_with: Steamed white basmati rice (Chelo) to soak up the rich sauce.

substitutions.note: Duck is the traditional meat used in Northern Iran. For a vegan option, use roasted butternut squash cubes.
Can be used to balance the tartness of the pomegranate molasses.
